nuggets.headline: Daft Punk's secret weapon was Thomas Bangalter's disco-producer father
nuggets.text: Thomas Bangalter’s father, Daniel Vangarde, was a successful disco producer behind 70s hits like Ottawan’s “D.I.S.C.O.” Vangarde's industry experience proved invaluable, as he guided the duo in navigating their early career and helped them secure the crucial deal with Virgin Records that launched *Homework* globally.

source.title: Daft Punk - Homework, The Blueprint That Rewrote Electronic Music's DNA - Decoded Magazine
source.quoteSnippet: His father, Daniel Vangarde, was a successful disco producer and songwriter... Vangarde proved instrumental in helping the young duo navigate their early career, particularly when it came to securing their crucial deal with Virgin Records.

nuggets.headline: Daft Punk mixed 'Around the World' on a JVC boombox from 1986
nuggets.text: The entirety of *Homework* was produced in Thomas Bangalter’s childhood bedroom on modest equipment. The final mixdowns, including this track, were done on an old JVC boombox Bangalter had owned since he was 11. They credited this unconventional monitor for the album's distinctive lo-fi warmth and punch.

source.title: Around the World (Daft Punk song)
source.quoteSnippet: Final mixing occurred on an old JVC boombox that Bangalter had owned since age 11, which the duo credited for imparting a distinctive lo-fi warmth to both Homework and their follow-up Discovery.

nuggets.imageCaption: Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo in their pre-robot, Homework era.

nuggets.headline: Daft Punk's 'Teachers' track shouts out an artist they 'creatively repurposed'
nuggets.text: On *Homework*, the track "Teachers" is a roll call of Daft Punk's influences. One name mentioned is Scottish producer Neil Landstrumm, whose track "Sniff And Destroy" bears a striking resemblance to their own raw, distorted banger, "Rollin' & Scratchin'". For a taste of the acidic, grinding techno that informed *Homework*'s harder edges, listen to Landstrumm's work from that same mid-90s period.

source.title: Daft Punk’s ‘Homework’ at 25: Teachers and Students | DJ Mag
source.quoteSnippet: To take one example: malevolent 4am favourite ‘Rollin & Scratchin’’ bears blatant similarities to ‘Sniff And Destroy’, made by Neil Landstrumm, a listed Teacher.

nuggets.imageCaption: A collage of artists named on the track 'Teachers,' including Neil Landstrumm.

artistSummary: The French duo of Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo, Daft Punk redefined electronic music by fusing analog warmth with digital precision. Their work filtered disco, funk, and house through a futuristic lens, creating a sound that was both reverent of the past and a blueprint for the future.
